title: Kemal Özkırşehirli
description: Academic homepage for Kemal Özkırşehirli: AI/ML for science, chemical physics, computational chemistry, geometric deep learning, CADD, protein design, chromatin biology, statistical mechanics, and scientific workflow engineering.

author.name: Kemal Özkırşehirli
author.role: MIT B.Sc. candidate · triple major in Computer Science and Engineering, Chemical Physics, and Mathematical Biology · minors in Philosophy and Writing
author.bio: I am an international student at MIT, and I hail from İstanbul, Türkiye. At MIT, I am a B.Sc. candidate expected May 2027 with a 5.0/5.0 GPA, pursuing a rare triple major in Computer Science and Engineering, Chemical Physics, and Mathematical Biology with minors in Philosophy and Writing. I am on track to graduate a year early, completing three majors and two minors in three total years of undergraduate education after transferring from Columbia University. I work at the intersection of AI/ML, chemical physics, computational chemistry, molecular simulation, CADD, protein and genome modeling, geometric deep learning, statistical mechanics, and scientific workflow engineering. My goal is to turn difficult scientific systems into computable representations, testable algorithms, and decision-making tools for discovery.
highlights: Current work spans TBXT CADD, V2M autoimmune target discovery, PRISM protein-model red-teaming, ChromoGen-V2, 3D mesh methodology for life-science fit, ChemAgent-QSM, EVEdesign, antibody RL, Pedal AI chemistry-agent infrastructure, and ML-augmented renormalization group theory.

Research and engineering across MIT Chemistry, MIT CSAIL, MIT Physics, A*STAR EDDC, W2D2/PRISM, Harvard Medical School, Columbia, AI@MIT, Weizmann Institute, MIT BioMakers, Pedal AI, Sepal AI/Mercor, and Buchwald Lab contexts.

Bronze medalist at IChO and IMChO; Columbia Science Research Fellow and Likely Letter recipient; TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad silver medalist; Waterloo distinction recipient; AIME qualifier with AMC 12 Hall of Fame recognition; YC Startup School 2026 accepted.

selectedSections.title: Molecular Simulation, CADD, and Chemical Physics

items.title: TBXT
items.description: Apr 2026–present chordoma/TBXT CADD sprint; docking, GNINA CNN rescoring, QSAR, BRICS generation, Boltz-2 co-folding, MMGBSA/FEP scaffolding, paralog selectivity, and public research release

items.title: ChemAgent-QSM
items.description: Jan–May 2026 LLM-guided quantum/statistical-mechanics pipelines using PySCF; TCFs, local order, relaxation, mobility, optimized geometries, and spectra

items.title: Renormalization Group
items.description: Jan 2022–present GNN/PyTorch Kadanoff RG framework for spin systems, fixed points, phase boundaries, and statistical-mechanics simulation [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: Kupcinet-Getz
items.description: Jun–Aug 2025 computational biochemical diffusion at Weizmann; stochastic reaction-diffusion modeling, PINN/neural-ODE solvers, Gillespie SSA, nonlinear oscillations, and trajectory analysis [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: Buchwald Lab
items.description: Dec 2025–May 2026 advanced organic synthesis in MIT Chemistry; CuH-catalyst development, asymmetric methylation/alkylation, NMR/HPLC analysis, and substrate-scope expansion

items.title: Pedal AI
items.description: Jul–Dec 2025 Python/LangGraph backend and chemistry-agent workflow engineering; stateful tool orchestration, molecular-prediction API integration, retrosynthesis-route UX, and cheminformatics workflow design [code not public]

items.title: Sepal AI / Mercor
items.description: Sept 2024–Feb 2025 chemistry LLM evaluation, rubric design, retrosynthesis/reaction-mechanism task construction, prompt analysis, and failure-mode annotation

items.title: DFT → kMC
items.description: Aug 2023–Feb 2024 multi-scale computational organic chemistry pipeline; transition-state screening, reaction-trajectory analysis, solvent-pathway modeling, and predicted yield optimization [code soon-to-be released]

selectedSections.title: Biomolecular AI, Protein, and Genome Modeling

items.title: 3D Meshes
items.description: Jun 2026–present methodology-first geometric deep learning collaboration for 3D meshes and point clouds; life-science applications are pursued if and only if the new method creates a principled scientific fit [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: ChromoGen V2
items.description: May 2026–present conditional diffusion for small-molecule-perturbed single-cell 3D chromatin ensembles with Plate-C/Easy Dip-C/CHARM-style data [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: PRISM
items.description: May 2026–present adversarial evaluation, reliability checks, uncertainty calibration, and failure taxonomies for protein foundation models [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: V2M
items.description: Apr 2026–present autoimmune genetics-to-mechanism target-discovery engine with single-cell foundation models, QTL/colocalization, targetability checks, and therapeutic-control validation [code soon-to-be released]

items.title: EVEdesign
